messages.content:
You are comparing two analyst responses to determine which better demonstrates knowledge of a specific finding from a longitudinal dataset.

Question asked: What actions should the team take to prevent this from recurring?

Finding to evaluate: connection pool exhaustion

Response A:
**Root‑cause clues from the logs**

| Symptom | Where it appears | Why it matters |
|---------|------------------|----------------|
| **Disk‑usage warnings on the metrics DB** – 78 % (01 ep 001), 82 % (01 ep 002), 79 % (01 ep 003) | `[cascading_failure_01_ep_001]`, `[cascading_failure_01_ep_002]`, `[cascading_failure_01_ep_003]` | When the DB runs out of space the write‑ahead log stalls, causing request‑time spikes and downstream time‑outs. |
| **SAML assertion / OIDC discovery errors** – 12 errors (01 dx 008), 12 errors (01 dx 006), 17 errors (01 dx 006) | `[cascading_failure_01_dx_006]`, `[cascading_failure_01_dx_008]` | Broken assertions generate “break‑glass” alerts and force retries that cascade into higher latency and pool exhaustion. |
| **LDAP bind timeout on directory sync** – “run #9 failed” | `[cascading_failure_01_ep_003]` | A failed sync blocks the audit pipeline (the pipeline later shows back‑pressure alerts). |
| **Audit‑pipeline drop‑rate & ingest‑lag warnings** – drop‑rate approaching threshold, p95 ingest lag 6.2 s, queue peak 3 180 events | `[cascading_failure_01_dx_008]` | When the pipeline backs up, downstream services (e.g., token‑ops, MFA) see higher error rates. |
| **Connection‑pool exhaustion** – idp‑api pools show exhaustion 2 / 1, avg_wait >10 ms | `[cascading_failure_01_dx_006]` | Exhausted pools force queuing, inflating p95/p99 latencies (e.g., /sso_sessions p99 210 ms). |
| **MFA‑enrollment error spikes** – 12 % (01 dx 001), 12.4 % (01 dx 003

Response B:


Which response better demonstrates awareness of this finding? The response may use different terminology. Focus on whether the core insight is present, not exact wording.

Respond with ONLY one word: A, B, or TIE
/no_think
